title: Known issues and release notes draft
summary: Draft M7 known issues and external-test release notes from verified M6/M7 evidence without hiding blockers or overclaiming readiness.
acceptance: Drafts known issues and external-test release notes from verified M6/M7 evidence
Records Web/Windows blockers, diagnostics limitations, save policy risks and player-support boundaries
Includes explicit non-goals for public release, store submission, signing, telemetry, accounts, paid services and M8
Does not hide failed gates, unresolved blockers or owner-gated decisions
Produces a release-notes package that WM-0108 and WM-0109 can cite

history.details: Created by WM-0098 M7 planning DAG; remains proposed until dependencies are done.

objective: Create truthful known-issues and release-note drafts for controlled external testing and public playtest evaluation.
forbiddenPaths: test behavior changes to hide failures
public release upload store submission signing installer or updater
telemetry accounts paid services crash upload or public feedback system
final privacy legal store or public save compatibility claims
M8 task creation, promotion, claim, implementation or review
risksAndFileOwnership: Risk: known issues can soften blockers; preserve exact blocker language from dependency reports.
Risk: release notes can read as release approval; keep them draft/external-test notes.
File ownership is limited to known-issues/release-note docs and WM-0107 reports.
ownerGate: Block if any unresolved blocker is omitted or downgraded without reviewed evidence
Block on release upload, store submission, signing, telemetry, accounts, paid services or final public claims
Block if dependency evidence is missing or unverified
m0M6RegressionCoverage: No runtime, save, platform verdict or benchmark threshold changes
Known issues must cite M6 Web demo-only and Windows controlled-test verdicts
M5 hashes and M0-M6 regression status remain protected
reviewRouting: qa-performance completes to reviewer; reviewer verifies blocker fidelity, dependency evidence and no readiness overclaim.
rollbackModel: Revert known-issues/release-note docs and WM-0107 reports; keep downstream protocol/readiness tasks blocked until replacement notes exist.

sparkEligibility.reason: Known issues and release notes consolidate milestone risk and public-facing readiness language.
